title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
Low Risk High Return Investments
The different types of investment options available in the market have different risk profiles. Some investment options have high risks as they are prone to market volatility. However, they have the potential of delivering attractive returns on your investment. On the other hand, some investment options have a low risk profile. However, in such cases, their returns might be modest. What if you want to invest in low-risk investments but want a higher return potential? Is it possible?
Yes, you can enjoy attractive returns with low-risk investments too. The trick is to choose the right investment options and manage your investments smartly. Let’s understand how to find and use low risk high return investments.
How to Get the Highest Returns at the Lowest Risk?
Here are some ways in which you can get good returns from your savings without exposing them to very high risks -
Diversification
Diversification is a type of investment strategy that aims to reduce investment risks by mixing different types of investment options. This limits the exposure to a single type of asset class and reduces the overall volatility. The objective behind diversification is to achieve good long-term returns on investments while minimising the risks of holding any one type of investment avenue.
So, you can choose equity, debt, gold, and other types of asset classes in your portfolio and diversify it to reduce risks while maximising the return potential.
Risk-Tolerance
Risk tolerance means your capacity to handle investment risks. You need to assess your risk appetite before selecting investment avenues. Once you know how much risk you can handle, you can choose investment options that align with your risk profile.
For instance, if you have a high risk appetite, you can allocate a larger proportion of your savings to equity-oriented investment options. On the other hand, if you do not prefer risks, you can keep equity exposure limited and enhance the debt exposure of your portfolio.
Investment Horizon
Investment horizon means the period for which you can stay invested. The longer the investment horizon, the higher your capacity to take risks. Moreover, with time, investment risks average out, and you can earn good returns.
Research and Expert Advice
You can also research the market to find investment options that have a low risk profile but offer attractive returns. If you want, you can also take the help of professional investment advisors to pick out investment avenues that match your risk profile, investment tenure, and financial goals.
Best Low Risk High Return Investment Options
Some low risk high return investment options that you can explore include the following -
High-Interest Savings Account
A savings account offers fixed interest on the balance maintained in the account. As such, there is no risk of market volatility. Moreover, if you choose accounts offering higher interest rates, you can enjoy attractive returns on your savings.
Annuities
Life insurance annuity plans can also offer good returns with low risks. If you choose traditional deferred annuity plans, you can create a stable retirement corpus which is not exposed to market risks. If the plan is issued as a with-profit or participating plan, you can earn reversionary bonuses that further enhance the retirement corpus. After vesting, you can receive guaranteed annuities from such plans, which would not be affected by market volatility.
Deferred annuity plans have some form of market risk. However, if you choose debt-oriented funds, the risks are reduced while you can still enjoy life insurance coverage with market-linked returns.
Under immediate annuity plans, there is no scope for market risk. You invest a lump sum amount and then start getting guaranteed annuity payouts lifelong.
Money Market Mutual Fund
A money market fund is a debt-oriented mutual fund scheme which invests a major portion of its portfolio in debt instruments having a short-term maturity period of up to one year. Since money market funds are debt funds, they have low risk and can offer stable market-linked returns.
Municipal Bonds
Municipal bonds are a type of bond instrument issued by the local or urban government or agencies. The bonds are issued to raise financing for infrastructure and other needs. The bonds have a maturity period of 3 years and offer market-linked returns.
Certificate of Deposits
A Certificate of Deposit (CD) is a fixed-income instrument issued by RBI (Reserve Bank of India) permitted banks, and financial institutions. The CD can be offered at a discount to its face value and offers assured returns to investors.
Debt-focused Unit Linked Insurance Plans (ULIPs)
ULIPs offer different types of investment funds to policyholders. Debt-focused ULIP funds are those that invest a major part of their portfolio in debt instruments like CDs, bonds, treasury bills, money market instruments, etc.
Debt funds have a low risk profile and can offer stable returns in a volatile market.
Treasury Bills
Treasury bills are a type of debt security issued by the government to raise short-term funds. These instruments have negligible risk as they are government-backed and are offered at a discount to their face value. They have a short maturity tenure, making them suitable for short-term investors.
Fixed Deposits
Fixed deposits are investment schemes offered by banks and financial institutions. Under FDs, you can deposit a lump sum amount of money for a specific period and get a fixed rate of interest on your deposit. FDs offer guaranteed returns and are hence not prone to volatility risks.
Corporate Bonds
Corporate bonds are debt securities that are issued by companies to raise funds for their business activities. These bonds can offer a fixed rate of interest to depositors. Many bonds are also listed on the stock exchange, making it easier for you to sell them before their maturity date.
Preferred Stocks
Preferred stocks or preference shares represent equity ownership in a company. These shares have preferential rights to the company’s earnings and assets and might earn a fixed dividend irrespective of the actual dividend rate. Compared to normal equity shares, preference shares have a lower risk profile.
PPF (Public Provident Fund)
PPF is a fixed-income, long-term investment plan that is also backed by the government. PPF offers fixed interest rates, which are revised periodically and hence are not exposed to market risks. You can earn assured returns on your investments, which are also tax-free in your hands.
Gold
Gold has a strategic long-term investment avenue as it is quite liquid, has no credit risk, is not abundantly available, and preserves its value over time. Gold is considered a safe haven in times of economic crisis and adds diversification to your portfolio.
National Savings Certificate (NSC)
The NSC is a fixed-income, low-risk investment option that offers fixed returns on your savings. It is backed by the government and has negligible investment risks. You can invest in the NSC scheme for a period of 5 years with a minimum amount starting from ₹1000. The investment also enjoys tax deduction under Section 80C in the old tax regime, making it tax-efficient.
National Pension System (NPS)
The NPS scheme is a retirement-oriented savings scheme introduced by the government. The scheme allows you to invest in market-linked funds and create a retirement corpus over time. Though the returns are not guaranteed, the NPS scheme allows debt funds to help minimise the risks.
Benefits of Choosing Low Risk High Return Investments
Some of the benefits of choosing investment plans that offer high returns and carry a low risk profile are as follows -
Capital Preservation
Capital preservation basically means preserving what you have invested. In other words, it means keeping your invested amount safe, irrespective of market movements. With low-risk investments, the chances of market volatility and the loss of capital are low. As such, your investment stays safe.
Steady Income
You can get stable returns on your investments when you choose low-risk avenues. These returns can be availed as regular income to supplement your regular source of income. For instance, non-cumulative FDs give out the interest income in the form of regular, steady income.
Reduced Stress
With low-risk investments, you don’t have to stress about incurring losses if the markets experience short-term fluctuations or volatility. This reduces the stress of investment risks.
Diversification Benefits
For aggressive investors, low-risk investments can add a touch of stability and diversify their portfolio across debt assets. This balances out the equity risks and helps investors enjoy the benefit of diversification.
Accessibility for Beginners
For investors who are new to investing, low-risk investments can be a suitable avenue to earn returns without facing the risk of capital loss.
Ideal for Short-Term Goals
If you are planning for short-term goals, you might want to preserve your investments and avoid investment risks. As such, low-risk, high-return investments can help you generate returns on your savings in the short run without high market risks.
Key Takeaways
Low-risk, high-return investments are those avenues that can offer attractive returns on investments without high investment risks.
There are various low-risk, high-return investment options like annuities, bonds, FDs, PPFs, preferred stocks, money market funds, debt-focused ULIPs, treasury bills, etc.
To maximise returns while minimising risks, try to diversify your portfolio, assess your risk appetite and investment horizon, and take professional advice if needed.
The benefits of low-risk high-return investments include capital protection, good returns, suitability for short-term goals, diversification, etc.
Conclusion
When choosing an investment avenue, assess your risk profile and financial goals and then make your decision. If you are risk-averse, low-risk investment options can be a suitable fit. Compare the different types of low-risk investment avenues available and choose those that can provide high returns to grow your savings. Research is important as it can help you understand an investment avenue, its return potential, and risk profile. So, research before investing and, if needed, you can seek professional assistance to invest your hard-earned money optimally.
FAQs
Which investment is the safest while offering the highest returns?
The choice of the safest investment option depends on your preference. For some investors, FDs can prove the safest, while others might prefer a PPF account. So, assess your financial goals and investment tenure and then choose a safe investment avenue that can deliver attractive returns.
What qualifies as a low-risk investment?
A low-risk investment is one that has limited risk exposure to market volatility. Such investments tend to generate stable returns on your savings even when the markets are fluctuating.
Is there any investment that is 100% safe?
No investment is 100% safe. Every investment involves some or the other form of risk. For instance, FDs might offer guaranteed returns, but they are prone to credit risk, reinvestment risk, liquidity risk, etc.
Disclaimer
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale. full_pdf_text:
Low Risk Investments
The different types of investment options available in the market have different risk profiles. Some investment options have high risks as they are prone to market volatility. However, they have the potential of delivering attractive returns on your investment. On the other hand, some investment options have a low risk profile. However, in such cases, their returns might be modest. You can choose such investment avenues if you want to avoid investment risks. . The trick is to choose the right investment options and manage your investments smartly. Let’s understand how to find and use low risk high return investments.
How to Get the Maximum Returns at the Lowest Risk?
Here are some ways in which you can get good returns from your savings without exposing them to very high risks -
1. Diversification
Diversification is a type of investment strategy that aims to reduce investment risks by mixing different types of investment options. This limits the exposure to a single type of asset class and reduces the overall volatility. The objective behind diversification is to achieve good long-term returns on investments while minimising the risks of holding any one type of investment avenue. So, you can choose between a variety of asset classes such as equity, debt, gold, and other types in your portfolio and diversify it to reduce risks while maximising the return potential. 2. Risk-Tolerance
Risk tolerance means your capacity to handle investment risks. You need to assess your risk appetite before selecting investment avenues. Once you know how much risk you can handle, you can choose investment options that align with your risk profile. For instance, if you have a high risk appetite, you can allocate a larger proportion of your savings to equity-oriented investment options. On the other hand, if you do not prefer risks, you can keep equity exposure limited and enhance the debt exposure of your portfolio. When you invest according to your risk appetite, you can avoid surprises and expect the returns that you might get. 3. Investment Horizon
Investment horizon means the period for which you can stay invested. The longer the investment horizon, the better would be the effect of compounding and you can earn good returns. 4. Research and Expert Advice
You can also research the market to find investment options that have a low risk profile but offer attractive returns. If you want, you can also take the help of professional investment advisors to pick out investment avenues that match your risk profile, investment tenure, and financial goals.
Low Risk Investment Options
Some low risk high return investment options that you can explore include the following -
1. High-Interest Savings Account
A savings account offers fixed interest on the balance maintained in the account. As such, there is no risk of market volatility. Moreover, if you choose accounts offering higher interest rates, you can enjoy attractive returns on your savings. 2. Annuities
Life insurance annuity plans can also offer returns with low risks. If you choose deferred annuity plans the annuity payments don’t start immediately after you deposit a lump‑sum amount. Instead, they begin at a later date chosen by you. Deferred annuities have two distinct phases — an accumulation phase where your corpus grows over time, and an income phase where payouts begin. On the other hand, immediate annuity plans start paying out shortly after a lump‑sum payment is made, making them suitable for those who need a predictable, immediate income stream. 3. Money Market Mutual Fund
A money market fund is a debt-oriented mutual fund scheme which invests a major portion of its portfolio in debt instruments having a short-term maturity period of up to one year1. Since money market funds are debt funds, they have low risk and can offer stable market- linked returns. 4. Municipal Bonds2
Municipal bonds are a type of bond instrument issued by the local or urban government or agencies. The bonds are issued to raise financing for infrastructure and other needs. The bonds have a maturity period of 3 years and offer market-linked returns. 5. Certificate of Deposits
A Certificate of Deposit (CD) is a fixed-income instrument issued by RBI (Reserve Bank of India) permitted banks, and financial institutions. The CD can be offered at a discount to its face value and offers assured returns to investors. 6. Debt-focused Unit Linked Insurance Plans (ULIPs)
ULIPs offer different types of market linked investment funds to policyholders. Debt-focused ULIP funds are those that invest a major part of their portfolio in debt instruments like bonds, treasury bills, money market instruments, etc. Debt funds have a low risk profile and can offer stable returns in a volatile market. 7. Treasury Bills
Treasury bills are a type of debt security issued by the government to raise short-term funds. These instruments have negligible risk as they are government-backed . They have a short maturity tenure, making them suitable for short-term investors. 8. Fixed Deposits
Fixed deposits are investment schemes offered by banks and financial institutions. Under FDs, you can deposit a lump sum amount of money for a specific period and get a fixed rate of interest on your deposit. FDs offer guaranteed returns and are hence not prone to volatility risks. 9. Corporate Bonds
Corporate bonds are debt securities that are issued by companies to raise funds for their business activities. These bonds can offer a fixed rate of interest to depositors. Many bonds are also listed on the stock exchange, making it easier for you to sell them before their maturity date. 10. Preferred Stocks
Preferred stocks or preference shares represent ownership in a company. These shares have preferential rights to the company’s earnings and assets and might earn a fixed dividend irrespective of the actual dividend rate.. 11. PPF (Public Provident Fund)
PPF is a fixed-income, long-term investment plan that is also backed by the government. PPF offers fixed interest rates, which are revised periodically and are not exposed to market risks. You can earn assured returns on your investments, which are also tax-free in your hands. 12. Gold3
Gold has a strategic long-term investment avenue as it is quite liquid, has no credit risk, preserves its value over time. Gold is considered a safe haven in times of economic crisis and adds diversification to your portfolio. 13. National Savings Certificate (NSC)4
The NSC is a fixed-income, low-risk investment option that offers fixed returns on your savings. It is backed by the government and has negligible investment risks. You can invest in the NSC scheme for a period of 5 years with a minimum amount starting from ₹1000. The investment also enjoys tax deduction under Section 80C in the old tax regime, making it tax- efficient. 14. National Pension System (NPS)
The NPS scheme is a retirement-oriented savings scheme introduced by the government. The scheme allows you to invest in market-linked funds and create a retirement corpus over time. Though the returns are not guaranteed, the NPS scheme allows debt funds to help minimise the risks.
Benefits of Choosing Low Risk Investments
Some of the benefits of choosing investment plans that offer high returns and carry a low risk profile are as follows -
1. Capital Preservation
Capital preservation basically means preserving what you have invested. In other words, it means keeping your invested amount safe, irrespective of market movements. With low-risk investments, the chances of market volatility and the loss of capital are low. As such, your investment stays safe. 2. Steady Income
You can get stable returns on your investments when you choose low-risk avenues. These returns can be availed as regular income to supplement your regular source of income. For instance, non-cumulative FDs give out the interest income in the form of regular, steady income. 3. Reduced Stress
With low-risk investments, you don’t have to stress about incurring losses if the markets experience short-term fluctuations or volatility. This reduces the stress of investment risks. 4. Diversification Benefits
For aggressive investors, low-risk investments can add a touch of stability and diversify their portfolio across debt assets. This balances out the equity risks and helps investors enjoy the benefit of diversification. 5. Accessibility for Beginners
For investors who are new to investing, low-risk investments can be a suitable avenue to earn returns without facing the risk of capital loss. 6. Considered for Short-Term Goals
If you are planning for short-term goals, you might want to preserve your investments and avoid investment risks. As such, low-risk, high-return investments can help you generate returns on your savings in the short run without high market risks.
Key Takeaways
1. Low-risk investments are those avenues that can offer returns on investments without high
investment risks. 2. There are various low-risk, high-return investment options like annuities, bonds, FDs, PPFs,
preferred stocks, money market funds, debt-focused ULIPs, treasury bills, etc. 3. To maximise returns while minimising risks, try to diversify your portfolio, assess your risk
appetite and investment horizon, and take professional advice if needed. 4. The benefits of low-risk investments include capital protection, good returns, suitability for
short-term goals, diversification, etc. Conclusion
When choosing an investment avenue, assess your risk profile and financial goals and then make your decision. If you are risk-averse, low-risk investment options can be a suitable fit. Compare the different types of low-risk investment avenues available and choose those that can provide high
returns to grow your savings. Research is important as it can help you understand an investment avenue, its return potential, and risk profile. So, research before investing and, if needed, you can seek professional assistance to invest your hard-earned money optimally.
FAQs
1. Which investment is the safest while offering the highest returns?
The choice of the safest investment option depends on your preference. For some investors, FDs can prove the safest, while others might prefer a PPF account. So, assess your financial goals and investment tenure and then choose a safe investment avenue that can deliver attractive returns. 2. What qualifies as a low-risk investment?
A low-risk investment is one that has limited risk exposure to market volatility. Such investments tend to generate stable returns on your savings even when the markets are fluctuating. 3. Is there any investment that is 100% safe?
No investment is 100% safe. Every investment involves some or the other form of risk. For instance, FDs might offer guaranteed returns, but they are prone to credit risk, reinvestment risk (the risk of losing out on returns if the interest rates have dropped), liquidity risk (the risk of not being able to convert the investment into liquid funds when needed), etc.
Disclaimer
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy
document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Bajaj Life Insurance Limited (Formerly known as Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Limited) Reg. Office Address: Bajaj Insurance House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006. CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, call us on Customer Care No. 020-6712 1212, mail us on: customercare@bajajlife.com. IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract.
The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors.

The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions.
Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company.
The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
